CFG.HOST_DESC: Use the full Azure DevOps URL, for example https://dev.azure.com/your-org or https://server:8080/tfs/DefaultCollection.
CFG.AUTO_IMPORT_LIMIT_DESC: Maximum number of Azure DevOps work items to fetch for automatic backlog import. Max {{max}}.
CFG.AUTO_IMPORT_WIQL: Auto import WIQL query (Auto Import only)
CFG.AUTO_IMPORT_WIQL_DESC: Optional. When set, this WIQL query fully replaces the auto import query, so you control the scope, state filter and ordering (Scope above is ignored). Useful to filter by Iteration Path, Area Path or work item type, or to match custom done-state names. Leave empty to use the Scope setting. Use a flat WorkItems query, e.g. Select [System.Id] From WorkItems Where [System.TeamProject] = 'MyProject' AND [System.IterationPath] = 'MyProject\Sprint 1' AND [System.State] <> 'Closed'.

FORM_HELP:
Here you can configure Super Productivity to list Azure DevOps work items for a project in the task creation panel. They are shown as suggestions and link back to the work item.

In addition, you can automatically import open work items. Provide a Personal Access Token with work-item read access.
